vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but requires immediate revision based on visual evidence. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) appear to be combined in the yellow pot, but show signs of severe decline. The black pot containing the rabbit is identified as P3.

plant_audit.P2_P4: Severe chlorosis and necrosis; specimen appears non-viable.
plant_audit.P3: One primary leaf remains; significant yellowing and necrotic margins on the left-most leaf; rabbit anchor stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, granular, and compacted. No visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on soil surface; electronic sensor cables present in P3.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial leaf yellowing observed on P3.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Minor postural shift in P3 leaf; continued desiccation.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Leaf necrosis on P3 left-most leaf has progressed significantly.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Overall loss of turgor pressure across all specimens.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Stabilization of necrotic state; no new growth; system remains in a state of terminal decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of advanced physiological stress. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from likely root-zone desiccation or nutrient lockout, evidenced by the yellowing and browning of the leaf margins. The occupants of the yellow pot are effectively dead.
vision_report.anomalies: Electronic sensor hardware (cables/probes) is present in the soil, which may be contributing to root disturbance or soil compaction.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently failing. The Pothos (P3) is the only specimen showing any remaining chlorophyll, though it is rapidly succumbing to necrosis. The yellow pot contains only remnants of the original plantings, with no signs of life. The environment is dry and lacks the necessary moisture to support the current specimens.
